author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
verses:
kalau jaṁ vidvāṁsaḥ sphuṭam abhiyajante dyuti⋅bharād
akṛṣṇaṁ kṛṣṇāṅgaṁ makha⋅vidhibhir utkīrtanamayaiḥ
upāsyaṁ ca prāhur jam akhila⋅caturthāśrama⋅juṣāṁ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

caritraṁ tanvānaḥ priyam aghavadāhlādana⋅padaṁ
jayodghoṣaiḥ samyag⋅viracita⋅śacī⋅śoka⋅haraṇaḥ
udañcan⋅mārtaṇḍa⋅dyuti⋅hara⋅dukūlāñcita⋅kaṭiḥ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

apāraṁ kasyāpi praṇayi⋅jana⋅vṛndasya kutukī
rasa⋅stomaṁ hṛtvā madhuram upabhoktuṁ kam api jaḥ
ruciṁ svām āvavre dyutim iha tadīyāṁ prakaṭayan
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

anārādhyaḥ prītyā ciram asura⋅bhāva⋅praṇayināṁ
prapannānāṁ daivīṁ prakṛtim adhidaivaṁ trijagati
ajasraṁ jaḥ śrīmāñ jayati sahajānanda⋅madhuraḥ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

gatir jaḥ pauṇḍrāṇāṁ prakaṭita⋅navadvīpa⋅mahimā
bhavenālaŋkurvan bhuvana⋅mahitaṁ śrotriya⋅kulam
punāty aṅgīkārād bhuvi paramahaṁsāśrama⋅padaṁ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

mukhenāgre pītvā madhuram iha nāmāmṛta⋅rasaṁ
dṛśor dvārā jas taṁ vamati ghana⋅bāṣpāmbu⋅miṣataḥ
bhuvi premṇas tattvaṁ prakaṭayitum ullāsita⋅tanuḥ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

tanūm āviṣkurvan nava⋅puraṭa⋅bhāsaṁ kaṭi⋅lasat⋅
karaṅkālaṅkāras taruṇa⋅gaja⋅rājāñcita⋅gatiḥ
priyebhyo jaḥ śikṣāṁ diśati nija⋅nirmālya⋅rucibhiḥ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

smitālokaḥ śokaṁ harati jagatāṁ jasya parito
girāṁ tu prārambhaḥ kuśala⋅paṭalīṁ pallavayati
padālambaḥ kaṁ vā praṇayati nahi prema⋅nivahaṁ
sa devaś caitanyākṛtir atitarāṁ naḥ kṛpayatu

śacī⋅sūnoḥ kīrtiḥ stavaka⋅nava⋅saurabhya⋅niviḍaṁ
pumān jaḥ prītātmā paṭhati kila padyāṣṭakam idam
sa lakṣmīvān etaṁ nija⋅pada⋅saroje praṇayitāṁ
dadānaḥ kalyāṇīm anupadam abādhaṁ sukhayatu
en_translation:
In this age of Kali, learned persons worship Him—who is fair-complexioned but is Kṛṣṇa Himself—through sacrificial rites consisting of the loud chanting of His names. They declare Him to be the worshipable object for all who have accepted the renounced order of life. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

He performs dear pastimes that are a source of joy even for the sinful, perfectly removing the sorrow of Mother Śacī with sounds of victory. His waist is adorned with silken cloth that surpasses the effulgence of the rising sun. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

Eager to enjoy a certain boundless, sweet flood of mellows belonging to His loving devotees, He has stolen Their mood. He covered His own dark complexion and manifested Their golden effulgence here. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

Though difficult to worship with love for a long time by those attached to a demoniac mentality, He bestows a divine nature upon the surrendered, being the supreme deity in the three worlds. He is glorious and naturally sweet with bliss and constantly triumphs. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

He is the refuge of the people of Bengal and manifested the glories of Navadvīpa, adorning the community of learned priests glorified by the world. By accepting them, He purifies the status of the highest order of renunciants on earth. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

Having first drunk the sweet nectar of the Holy Name through His mouth, He emits it through the doors of His eyes under the guise of profuse tears. His body is thrilled to manifest the essence of divine love on earth. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

Manifesting a body with the effulgence of new gold, He is adorned at the waist with a water pot, and His gait is like that of a young elephant king. He gives instruction to His dear ones by the charm of His own remnants. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

His smiling glance completely takes away the sorrow of the worlds, and the very beginning of His speech causes hosts of auspiciousness to sprout. The shelter of His feet bestows floods of divine love upon everyone. May that Lord, in the form of Caitanya, bestow upon us His abundant mercy.

The glories of the son of Śacī are as dense as the fresh fragrance of a cluster of flowers. May that fortunate person who reads these eight verses with a joyful heart be made happy at every step with unobstructed auspiciousness, as the Lord bestows devotion at His own lotus feet.
